title: Mental Math Strategies for Subtraction
essential_question: What mental math strategies can you use to find differences?

pretest.question: Use mental math. 74 − 39 =
pretest.hint: Try subtracting 40 first, then adding back 1.

expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 Module 2 Lesson 4 Teacher Edition — 'Students frequently apply the compensation in the same direction as the original adjustment; explicit instruction that compensation must oppose the adjustment is required.'
expected_errors.note: D: 74−40=34, then subtracted 1 instead of adding → 33 (보정 방향 반전)

expected_errors.citation: Fuson et al. (1997) p.143 — 'Compensation errors frequently involve the correct direction but an incorrect magnitude; students lose track of the amount added when their working memory is occupied with the addition itself.'

expected_errors.note: C: subtracted too little; 74−29=45 or misread number

expected_errors.note: D: 93−30=63 but forgot to add 2 back → 63 (보정 생략, 방향 오류)

pretest.hint: 99 is close to 100. Subtract 100, then add 1 back.

expected_errors.citation: EngageNY G3 Module 2 Lesson 4 Teacher Edition — 'Students frequently apply the compensation in the same direction as the original adjustment; explicit instruction that compensation must oppose the adjustment is required.'

pretest.question: Ruby has 78 books. 31 are on shelves. How many are in boxes?
pretest.hint: Break 31 into 30 and 1. Subtract each part.

expected_errors.note: A: count-up hops miscounted: landed on 44 instead of 54
D: partial count-up (counted only ones, not tens): 4+2=6 → 76

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 9 p.39-40

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 4 — friendly-number subtraction and count-up mental strategies

assessment_source.name: Fuson et al. (1997) — mental strategy selection and compensation error patterns in 3-digit subtraction

learn.title: Strategy 1: Count Up
learn.content: Start at the smaller number. Count up by tens, then by ones, to reach the bigger number. Example: 62−39. Start at 39. Count up: 39→49→59 (that's +20), 59→60→61→62 (that's +3). So 62−39=23.
learn.math_note: count-up 전략: 뺄 수에서 위로 세기 — 601−598: 598→601=3

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 9 p.39-42

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 4 — friendly-number subtraction and count-up mental strategies

assessment_source.name: Fuson et al. (1997) — mental strategy selection and compensation error patterns in 3-digit subtraction

learn.title: Strategy 2: Take Away Tens and Ones
learn.content: Start at the bigger number. Subtract the tens, then the ones. Example: 62−39. Start at 62. Take away 30: 62→32. Take away 9: 32→23. So 62−39=23.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 9 p.39-42

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 4 — friendly-number subtraction and count-up mental strategies

assessment_source.name: Fuson et al. (1997) — mental strategy selection and compensation error patterns in 3-digit subtraction

learn.title: Strategy 3: Use Friendly Numbers
learn.content: Change the number you're subtracting to a 'friendly' number (like a multiple of 10). Example: 74−39. Think: 39 is close to 40. 74−40=34. But you subtracted 1 too many, so add 1 back: 34+1=35.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 9 p.39-42

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 4 — friendly-number subtraction and count-up mental strategies

assessment_source.name: Fuson et al. (1997) — mental strategy selection and compensation error patterns in 3-digit subtraction

learn.title: When to Use Each Strategy
learn.content: Count Up works best when the two numbers are close together (like 62−58). Take Away works well for any subtraction. Friendly Numbers work best when one number is close to a ten or hundred (like 99, 198, 301).
learn.math_note: 전략 선택 기준: 수가 가까우면 count-up; 99·98 → friendly numbers; 기타 → tens&ones

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 9 p.39-42

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 4 — friendly-number subtraction and count-up mental strategies

assessment_source.name: Fuson et al. (1997) — mental strategy selection and compensation error patterns in 3-digit subtraction

learn.title: Example: 285 − 99
learn.content: 99 is close to 100. Use friendly numbers: 285−100=185. You subtracted 1 too many, so add back 1: 185+1=186. Answer: 285−99=186.

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 9 p.39-42

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 4 — friendly-number subtraction and count-up mental strategies

assessment_source.name: Fuson et al. (1997) — mental strategy selection and compensation error patterns in 3-digit subtraction

learn.title: Number Line Visual
learn.content: A number line helps you see the jumps. For 74−39: Draw a line. Mark 39. Jump +1 to 40. Jump +30 to 70. Jump +4 to 74. Total jumps: 1+30+4=35.
learn.math_note: 수직선 시각화: 뺄셈=왼쪽 이동; count-up=오른쪽 이동으로 거리 측정

concept_source.name: Go Math Grade 3 Chapter 1 Lesson 9 p.39-42

procedure_source.name: EngageNY Grade 3 Module 2 Lesson 4 — friendly-number subtraction and count-up mental strategies

assessment_source.name: Fuson et al. (1997) — mental strategy selection and compensation error patterns in 3-digit subtraction

learn.math_talk_prompt: Look at the number line for 130 − 76. You can solve it by moving LEFT (subtracting 76), OR by moving RIGHT from 76 up to 130 (count-up). Both give the same answer. Which direction did YOU choose, and why? Is there a type of problem where moving right (count-up) is clearly faster than moving left? Give an example.

learn.title: Common Mistake Alert!
learn.content: When using friendly numbers, remember to ADJUST! If you subtract more than needed, ADD back. If you subtract less than needed, SUBTRACT more. Example: 74−39. Subtract 40 (1 too many) → add 1 back.
learn.math_note: 흔한 실수: friendly numbers 보정 방향 반전 (빼야 할 때 더하거나 그 반대)

lesson_summary: 암산으로 뺄셈을 풀 때는 세 가지 전략을 상황에 맞게 선택합니다: (1) 두 수가 가까우면 count-up, (2) 99·98처럼 10·100에 가까우면 friendly numbers, (3) 그 외에는 십·일 단위로 나누어 빼기. friendly numbers 보정은 반드시 반대 방향(더 빼면 +, 덜 빼면 −)으로 적용합니다.
unit_intro_message: 이 단원에서는 1,000 이내 덧셈·뺄셈을 암산 전략으로 풀고, 자릿값 알고리즘으로 검증합니다.
unit_close_message: 전략을 선택했으면 반드시 역연산으로 검증하세요: 뺄셈 후 빼는 수를 더했을 때 원래 수가 나오면 정답입니다.
